title: Policy Gradient with Adaptive Entropy Annealing for Continual Fine-Tuning
problem: Continual learning (CL) challenges, particularly the trade-off between stability (retaining past knowledge) and plasticity (acquiring new knowledge)

methodology.approach: Reformulating continual learning through a reinforcement learning (RL) lens
key_contributions: Expected Policy Gradient (EPG) - a low-variance variant of REINFORCE
methodology.formulation: Classification as a one-step Markov Decision Process (MDP)

continual_learning_approaches: Regularization (Buzzega et al., 2020; Rebuffi et al., 2017; Kirkpatrick et al., 2017)
Parameter isolation (Xu and Zhu, 2018; Mallya and Lazebnik, 2018; Hung et al., 2019)
peft_methods: LoRA (Liang and Li, 2024; Liu and Chang, 2025)
Adapters (Gao et al., 2024; Yu et al., 2024; Wang et al., 2025; Ermis et al., 2022)

key_insights.ce_limitations: Prioritizes harder samples but risks destabilizing prior knowledge through excessive exploration
key_insights.epg_advantages: More exploitative, emphasizing easier samples that align with model predictions
key_insights.entropy_dynamics: EPG consistently produces lower-entropy output distributions than CE

results.performance: aEPG improves performance across four CL benchmarks
results.compatibility: Works with multiple PEFT architectures (LoRA, Adapter, Prefix)
results.entropy_regularization: Low-entropy objectives consistently improve continual learning
